parsedContent.title: Primary 3 Science Exam
parsedContent.description: Assessing primary 3 students' knowledge in basic science concepts

questions.text: What is the process called when plants make their own food from sunlight?
Which part of the human body helps us to breathe?
Describe the water cycle and its importance to our planet.
questions.correctAnswer: The water cycle is the process by which water moves from the Earth to the atmosphere and back again. It is important because it helps to distribute water around the planet, which is necessary for life.

questions.text: What are the main parts of a plant and what are their functions?
questions.correctAnswer: The main parts of a plant are the roots, stem, leaves, and flowers. The roots absorb water and nutrients from the soil, the stem supports the plant and transports water and nutrients, the leaves make food through photosynthesis, and the flowers produce seeds.

questions.text: The sun is a star that is closest to the Earth.
What is the largest planet in our solar system?
The process of plants making their own food from sunlight is called ___
Match the following animals with their habitats: lion - ___ , fish - ___ , bird - ___
Put the following steps of the water cycle in order: evaporation, condensation, precipitation
